name.es: Cozonac (pan rumano con remolino de nueces)
name.fr: Cozonac (brioche roumaine au tourbillon de noix)

description.en: Romania's holiday centerpiece: a soft, egg-yellow yeasted loaf twisted around a walnut-cocoa filling before baking, so every slice reveals a dark marbled swirl. Cozonac appears at both Christmas and Easter, always cut thick and shared.
description.de: Rumäniens Festtagsgebäck: ein weicher, eigelbgelber Hefeteig, der vor dem Backen um eine Walnuss-Kakao-Füllung gedreht wird, sodass jede Scheibe eine dunkle Marmorierung zeigt. Cozonac kommt zu Weihnachten wie zu Ostern auf den Tisch.
description.es: El pan festivo de Rumanía: una masa de levadura suave y amarilla, retorcida antes de hornear alrededor de un relleno de nuez y cacao, de modo que cada rebanada muestra un remolino oscuro. El cozonac se sirve en Navidad y en Pascua.
description.fr: La brioche des fêtes en Roumanie : une pâte levée, moelleuse et dorée aux jaunes d'œuf, torsadée avant cuisson autour d'une garniture de noix et de cacao, si bien que chaque tranche révèle un tourbillon sombre. Servie à Noël et à Pâques.

origin.region: Romania and Moldova (also Bulgaria, as kozunak)
origin.note: A fixture of both the Christmas and Easter table; related sweet breads under other names span much of the former Ottoman Balkans.

ingredients.note: 13 g in the dough, 4 g in the filling

yield.shape: twisted double-strand loaf in a pan

keeping.note: Wrapped well, the buttery crumb stays moist for days; freezes nicely sliced for later.

method.title: Activate the yeast
method.summary: Warm the milk, stir in the yeast and a pinch of sugar, and let it foam for a few minutes.

method.title: Mix the dough
method.summary: Whisk egg yolks with sugar, vanilla, sour cream and orange zest, then combine with the yeasted milk, flour and salt. Work in the softened butter and knead to a soft, glossy dough; let it rise until doubled.

method.title: Make the filling
method.summary: Whisk egg whites with sugar, ground walnuts, cocoa and a little rum flavoring into a thick, spreadable paste.

method.title: Roll and fill
method.summary: Divide the risen dough, roll each piece into a rectangle, spread with the walnut-cocoa filling, and roll up tightly into a log.

method.title: Twist and pan
method.summary: Cut each log in half lengthwise and twist the two cut sides together so the filling spirals through, then settle the twist into a buttered loaf pan.

method.title: Second rise
method.summary: Cover and proof again until puffy and nearly to the rim of the pan.

method.title: Bake and cool
method.summary: Bake until deep gold and a skewer comes out clean, then cool fully before slicing to reveal the marbled swirl.

source.adaptation: Method paraphrased; added a small amount of orange zest to the dough as a common regional touch; formula percentages count the dough only, filling sugar/nuts excluded.
